---
title: Ghost
description: The Ghost publishing platform backed by its own MySQL database — a two-service template.
---

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

[Ghost](https://ghost.org) is a professional publishing platform — a blog and newsletter. This template
brings up Ghost together with its own **MySQL** database, wired together for you.

## What you get

| Service           | Image   | Role                                                 |
| ----------------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `ghost` (primary) | `ghost` | The Ghost app — gets the default domain.             |
| `db`              | `mysql` | A dedicated MySQL database with a persistent volume. |

The two database passwords (`db_password`, `db_root_password`) are **generated per instance** and shared
between the services automatically — you never type them.

## Inputs

- **`url`** (optional) — the public URL of your blog, e.g. `https://blog.example.com`. Ghost bakes this
  into generated links; leave it blank to get a default kubwave domain, or set it to the address you'll
  actually serve from.

## Deploy it

1. **Create Service → Ghost** in your environment.
2. Fill in **url** with your public blog address.
3. Create, then deploy both services. Once MySQL is ready and Ghost has migrated, open the URL to
   finish setup at `/ghost`.

<Aside type="caution">`url` is hard to change later — Ghost stores absolute links built from it. Set it to the final domain before going live.</Aside>
